content.claim: Hungary's early academic selection (ages 10-12) into 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks occurs far earlier than the OECD average (age 15), with around 85% of Hungarian students in schools permitted to select by academic performance compared to the OECD average of 43%.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: The 85% figure reflects schools with selection capacity, not the proportion of students actually selected into 6/8-year tracks
OECD comparability depends on consistent definitions of 'academic selection' across country systems
Aggregate tracking prevalence does not isolate the specific impact of the 6/8-year gimnázium model from other selective mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
